evals.name: fast-path-proceed
evals.expected_output: Runs in fast-path mode (no --adversarial flag). Phase 0 reads wip/plan_my-feature_*.md artifacts, detects input_type, selects fast-path execution mode. Phases 1-4 each use a single agent. Phase 5 synthesizes findings. If no critical findings, writes wip/plan_my-feature_review.md with verdict: proceed and empty critical_findings. Does not write a loopback file.
assertions: Fast-path mode is used (single agent per category)
Phase 0 reads all four required plan artifacts
Phase 5 writes exactly one verdict file
If verdict is proceed, the file is wip/plan_my-feature_review.md
The review_result YAML block includes verdict, loop_target, round, confidence, critical_findings, and summary fields

evals.name: adversarial-mode-flag
evals.expected_output: Detects --adversarial flag in arguments. Phase 0 selects adversarial execution mode. For each of phases 1-4, spawns three validator agents in parallel. After all validators complete, identifies agreements (>=2 validators flagged the same issue) and disagreements (only 1 validator flagged it). Runs cross-examination for each disagreement. Phase 5 synthesizes confirmed findings into a verdict artifact using the same review_result schema as fast-path.
assertions: The --adversarial flag is detected and adversarial mode is selected
Three validator agents are spawned per category (not one)
All validator agents run in parallel before cross-examination
Cross-examination agents are spawned for disagreements (findings flagged by only one validator)
The output review_result schema is identical to fast-path mode output

evals.name: loop-back-category-c
evals.expected_output: When Category C (AC Discriminability) finds fixture-anchored ACs, Phase 5 sets verdict to loop-back with loop_target: 4. Phase 5 writes wip/plan_my-feature_review_loopback.md (not _review.md). The loopback file contains the critical_findings with non-empty correction_hint for each Category C finding. Phase 6 deletes wip/ artifacts from Phase 4 forward (dependencies.md, manifest.json, issue_*.md) but does NOT delete the loopback file itself.
assertions: verdict is loop-back when critical findings exist
Category C findings produce loop_target: 4
The loopback file (not the review file) is written
All Category C findings include a non-empty correction_hint
Phase 6 deletes manifest.json and issue_*.md but preserves the loopback file

evals.name: roadmap-input-type
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 detects input_type: roadmap from the analysis artifact. Categories B, C, and D return empty critical_findings immediately (roadmap is exempt from these checks). Category A runs a restricted check: verifies issue count matches roadmap item count only. confidence is set to low due to roadmap input type. verdict is proceed if the count matches.
assertions: input_type: roadmap is detected from the analysis artifact
Categories B, C, and D return critical_findings: [] without running checks
Category A runs the restricted roadmap check (count only)
confidence is low for roadmap input
The verdict proceeds to Phase 5 synthesis with mostly empty findings

evals.name: sub-operation-round-precedence
evals.prompt: Called as sub-operation with args: plan_topic=my-feature, round=2, mode=fast-path
evals.expected_output: When called with args.round=2, the review_result block uses round: 2 regardless of the review_rounds value in the analysis artifact. Does not compute round from review_rounds + 1 when args.round is present. Runs in fast-path mode as specified by mode: fast-path.
assertions: round in the verdict file is 2 (from args.round), not derived from review_rounds
Fast-path mode is used as specified
The verdict file is written to the correct path for the given topic
